symptoms.symptom_name: Hyper-acute crop of hundreds of pustules over the friction/occlusion zones ~24 hours after a sweaty occlusive long run

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rapid crop of follicular pustules confined to friction/occlusion zones immediately after heavy sweating under tight athletic wear is the defining mechanism and time-course of occlusive/frictional folliculitis.
The hyper-acute 24-hour onset and calf/buttock distribution are atypical for acne, which is chronic and centro-facial/truncal.

symptoms.symptom_name: Monomorphic 1-3 mm pustules each centred precisely on a hair with an erythematous halo and normal intervening skin, no comedones

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pustules each centred on a hair follicle with a narrow erythematous halo and normal intervening skin are near-defining for a folliculitis and distinguish it from non-follicular pustular eruptions.
The absence of comedones and the strictly monomorphic follicular pustules argue against acne vulgaris.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus predominant over pain, preventing sleep

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Itch predominance with only mild tenderness fits a superficial folliculitis rather than a deep, painful abscess.
A deep abscess is dominated by severe localised pain rather than itch; itch predominance argues against a deep infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No deep tender fluctuant nodules, no spreading cellulitic erythema/heat, no lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deeper staphylococcal soft-tissue infection (furuncle/carbuncle/abscess or cellulitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of deep fluctuant nodules, spreading erythema/heat and lymphadenopathy makes an established abscess or cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target telehealth cannot palpate for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no chills, rigors or systemic malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete absence of systemic features makes invasive/systemic infection unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Monomorphic hair-centred pustules (not grouped vesicles/punched-out erosions), no mucosal sores, and no medications/new drug

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uniform hair-centred pustules without grouped vesicles, punched-out erosions or mucosal involvement argue strongly against disseminated HSV/eczema herpeticum, though the widespread vesiculopustular appearance mandates keeping it as a safety-net.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction (e.g. AGEP / other SCAR)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No medications and nothing started new, with an eruption correlating exactly to a mechanical trigger, argues strongly against a drug-induced pustulosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hot tub/spa/unchlorinated pool exposure; distribution follows friction/sweat zones not swimsuit occlusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of water exposure and a friction/sweat rather than swimsuit-occlusion distribution argue against a Pseudomonas 'hot tub' folliculitis.
The distribution mapping exactly to the tight-athletic-wear friction/sweat zones fits a mechanical/occlusive folliculitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 122/78, HR 82, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 75 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; normal, afebrile and haemodynamically stable vitals argue against systemic infection. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine GP review (in person or video) if not settling on treatment, to confirm folliculitis and exclude a deeper soft-tissue infection or early HSV
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct inspection of the pustules and palpation for deep infection are the assessments telehealth cannot fully do, and review is needed to obtain the topical antibiotic and reassess if the eruption does not improve.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab of a pustule for microscopy, culture and sensitivities (including MRSA) if extensive, recurrent or not responding
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides targeted therapy in a widespread or treatment-refractory folliculitis and identifies MRSA; not required acutely for a first, classically-triggered episode.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab/PCR of a lesion for HSV if grouped vesicles, punched-out erosions or systemic upset develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — the lesions are monomorphic hair-centred pustules, not grouped vesicles. Reserve for if a herpetic picture emerges.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Systemic bloods / blood cultures / septic screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No systemic features and a superficial folliculocentric eruption; systemic infection work-up would be over-investigation unless fever or systemic upset develops.
